These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}} == Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}} === Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}} == Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries. === Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun. === Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}. == Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage {{World Heritage Site summary table|o |site_img = File:NintokuTomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg |site_img_capt = {{ill|Nintoku-ryo Kofun|ja|仁徳陵古墳}}, part of the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}) |site_img_width = 275px |ja_name = 百舌鳥・古市古墳群
-古代日本の墳墓群- |en_name = {{lang|JA|[[:en:Mozu tombs|Mozu]]-[[:en:Furuichi kofungun|Furuichi]] [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan}} |fr_name = {{lang|fr|Ensemble de kofun de Mozu-Furuichi : tertres funéraires de l’ancien Japon}} |country = Japan |area = 166.66 [[Hectare|ha]]
（Buffer zone: 890 ha） |criterion_c = (3),(4) |criterion_n = |Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category = Sites |iucn = |rg_year = 2019 ({{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}}) |ex_rg_year = |Danger = |remarks = |url_no = 1593 |locmapin = Japan |relief = |lat_degrees = 34 |lat_minutes = 33 |lat_seconds = 50 |lat_direction = N |long_degrees = 135 |long_minutes = 29 |long_seconds = 14 |long_direction = E }} == Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group == '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}} == Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}} === Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}} == Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries. === Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun. === Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}. == Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}} == Composition == The site These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>

After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>

Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>

=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.

revision.comment: fixing various chatGPT errors and removing the new pages category
revision.text:
'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:2019 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:百舌鳥・古市古墳群]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja: [[ja:v]]

'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:2019 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:百舌鳥・古市古墳群]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja:v]]

revision.text:
'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:2019 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:百舌鳥・古市古墳群]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]]
== References == [[ja:v]]

== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:2019 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:百舌鳥・古市古墳群]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: inserting initial edit on these so the draft system sends me talk messages instead of dummy user
revision.text:
'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:2019 establishments in Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:百舌鳥・古市古墳群]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] == References == [[ja:v]]

== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:2019 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:百舌鳥・古市古墳群]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]]

'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:2019 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:百舌鳥・古市古墳群]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]]

'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese tumuli]] These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>

Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>

=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese tumuli]] == Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.

'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun === Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.

'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese tumuli]] '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese tumuli]] == Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.

'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019年7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019th year 7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019th year 7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019th year 7月6日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}} [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=draft}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019th year 7月6日|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁|12=draft}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=draft}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=draft}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=draft}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命|12=draft}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁|12=draft}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=draft}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019th year 7月6日|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁|12=draft}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=draft}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=draft}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=draft}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命|12=draft}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁|12=draft}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

'''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=draft}} meeting on {{ill|July 6, 2019|ja|2019th year 7月6日|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁|12=draft}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=draft}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=draft}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=draft}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=draft}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|ja|仲姫命|12=draft}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|ja|文化庁|12=draft}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|qid=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|qid=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|qid=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|qq=|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|qid=Q66124382}}; {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972}}; {{ill|Itasuke Kofun|qid=Q11259430}}; {{ill|Jōganjiyama Kofun|qid=Q66124384}}; {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|qid=Q11359978}}; {{ill|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11637613}}; {{ill|Komoyamazuka Kofun|qid=Q66124358}}; {{ill|Komuroyama Kofun|qid=Q11411025}}; {{ill|Kurizuka Kofun|qid=Q66124381}}; {{ill|Magodayūyama Kofun|qid=Q30925787}}; {{ill|Maruhoyama Kofun|qid=Q11368031}}; {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|qid=Q11476069}}; {{ill|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584}}; {{ill|Mukōhakayama Kofun|qid=Q11415923}}; {{ill|Nabezuka Kofun|qid=Q11650613}}; {{ill|Nagatsuka Kofun|qid=Q11651650}}; {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|qid=Q11549996}}; {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|qid=Q11378467}}; {{ill|Nakayamazuka Kofun|qid=Q66124374}}; {{ill|Nishiumazuka Kofun|qid=Q66124383}}; {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|qid=Q11645001}}; {{ill|Ojin-tenno-ryo Kofun, Konda-maruyama Kofun and Futatsuzuka Kofun|qid=Q66124378}}; {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|qid=Q11471921}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|qid=Q11410610}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|qid=Q66124366}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|qid=Q66124373}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|qid=Q11576219}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|qid=Q66124356}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|qid=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|qid=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|qid=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|qid=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|qid=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|qid=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|qid=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|qq=|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}
