{{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|qq=|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}}; {{ill|Sakai|WD=Q193428}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.5622|N|135.6094|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Aoyama Kofun|WD=Q66124385}}; {{ill|Daisen Kofun|WD=Q5849431}}; {{ill|Dōgameyama Kofun|WD=Q48751867}}; {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}; {{ill|Gen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124353}}; {{ill|Gobyōyama Kofun|WD=Q11489009}}; {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124371}}; {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|WD=Q11429120}}; {{ill|Hatazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124362}}; {{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|WD=Q11275423}}; {{ill|Haze Nisanzai Kofun|WD=Q11423449}}; {{ill|Higashiumazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124380}}; {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124382}}; {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972}}; {{ill|Itasuke Kofun|WD=Q11259430}}; {{ill|Jōganjiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124384}}; {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11359978}}; {{ill|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11637613}}; {{ill|Komoyamazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124358}}; {{ill|Komuroyama Kofun|WD=Q11411025}}; {{ill|Kurizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124381}}; {{ill|Magodayūyama Kofun|WD=Q30925787}}; {{ill|Maruhoyama Kofun|WD=Q11368031}}; {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|WD=Q11476069}}; {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}}; {{ill|Mukōhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11415923}}; {{ill|Nabezuka Kofun|WD=Q11650613}}; {{ill|Nagatsuka Kofun|WD=Q11651650}}; {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|WD=Q11549996}}; {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|WD=Q11378467}}; {{ill|Nakayamazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124374}}; {{ill|Nishiumazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124383}}; {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|WD=Q11645001}}; {{ill|Ojin-tenno-ryo Kofun, Konda-maruyama Kofun and Futatsuzuka Kofun|WD=Q66124378}}; {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11471921}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|WD=Q11410610}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|WD=Q66124366}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|WD=Q66124373}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|WD=Q11576219}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11471921}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|WD=Q11410610}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|WD=Q66124366}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|WD=Q66124373}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|WD=Q11576219}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972}}; {{ill|Itasuke Kofun|WD=Q11259430}}; {{ill|Jōganjiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124384}}; {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11359978}}; {{ill|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11637613}}; {{ill|Komoyamazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124358}}; {{ill|Komuroyama Kofun|WD=Q11411025}}; {{ill|Kurizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124381}}; {{ill|Magodayūyama Kofun|WD=Q30925787}}; {{ill|Maruhoyama Kofun|WD=Q11368031}}; {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|WD=Q11476069}}; {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}}; {{ill|Mukōhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11415923}}; {{ill|Nabezuka Kofun|WD=Q11650613}}; {{ill|Nagatsuka Kofun|WD=Q11651650}}; {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|WD=Q11549996}}; {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|WD=Q11378467}}; {{ill|Nakayamazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124374}}; {{ill|Nishiumazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124383}}; {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|WD=Q11645001}}; {{ill|Ojin-tenno-ryo Kofun, Konda-maruyama Kofun and Futatsuzuka Kofun|WD=Q66124378}}; {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11471921}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|WD=Q11410610}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|WD=Q66124366}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|WD=Q66124373}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|WD=Q11576219}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972}}; {{ill|Itasuke Kofun|WD=Q11259430}}; {{ill|Jōganjiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124384}}; {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11359978}}; {{ill|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11637613}}; {{ill|Komoyamazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124358}}; {{ill|Komuroyama Kofun|WD=Q11411025}}; {{ill|Kurizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124381}}; {{ill|Magodayūyama Kofun|WD=Q30925787}}; {{ill|Maruhoyama Kofun|WD=Q11368031}}; {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|WD=Q11476069}}; {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}}; {{ill|Mukōhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11415923}}; {{ill|Nabezuka Kofun|WD=Q11650613}}; {{ill|Nagatsuka Kofun|WD=Q11651650}}; {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|WD=Q11549996}}; {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|WD=Q11378467}}; {{ill|Nakayamazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124374}}; {{ill|Nishiumazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124383}}; {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|WD=Q11645001}}; {{ill|Ojin-tenno-ryo Kofun, Konda-maruyama Kofun and Futatsuzuka Kofun|WD=Q66124378}}; {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11471921}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|WD=Q11410610}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|WD=Q66124366}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|WD=Q66124373}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|WD=Q11576219}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>

=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972}}; {{ill|Itasuke Kofun|WD=Q11259430}}; {{ill|Jōganjiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124384}}; {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11359978}}; {{ill|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11637613}}; {{ill|Komoyamazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124358}}; {{ill|Komuroyama Kofun|WD=Q11411025}}; {{ill|Kurizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124381}}; {{ill|Magodayūyama Kofun|WD=Q30925787}}; {{ill|Maruhoyama Kofun|WD=Q11368031}}; {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|WD=Q11476069}}; {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11471921}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|WD=Q11410610}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|WD=Q66124366}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|WD=Q66124373}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|WD=Q11576219}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11471921}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|WD=Q11410610}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|WD=Q66124366}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|WD=Q66124373}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|WD=Q11576219}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>

Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>

=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.

{{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11471921}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|WD=Q11410610}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|WD=Q66124366}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|WD=Q66124373}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|WD=Q11576219}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], and [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]]. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]]. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to [[:en:Nakatsuhime|Empress Nakatsuhime]].
== Related Information == * [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)]] official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}
== References ==

{{ill|Komoyamazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124358}}; {{ill|Komuroyama Kofun|WD=Q11411025}}; {{ill|Kurizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124381}}; {{ill|Magodayūyama Kofun|WD=Q30925787}}; {{ill|Maruhoyama Kofun|WD=Q11368031}}; {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|WD=Q11476069}}; {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}}; {{ill|Mukōhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11415923}}; {{ill|Nabezuka Kofun|WD=Q11650613}}; {{ill|Nagatsuka Kofun|WD=Q11651650}}; {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|WD=Q11549996}}; {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|WD=Q11378467}}; {{ill|Nakayamazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124374}}; {{ill|Nishiumazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124383}}; {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|WD=Q11645001}}; {{ill|Ojin-tenno-ryo Kofun, Konda-maruyama Kofun and Futatsuzuka Kofun|WD=Q66124378}}; {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11471921}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|WD=Q11410610}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|WD=Q66124366}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|WD=Q66124373}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|WD=Q11576219}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524104]] Wikidata name: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|en|Nakatsuhime|WD=Q11378496|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}
== References ==

{{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259}}}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|en|Nakatsuhime|WD=Q11378496|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}
== References ==

== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|en|Nakatsuhime|WD=Q11378496|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}
== References ==

{{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356|1=Ryusa-yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365|1=Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375|1=Yashimazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367|1=Zen'emon'yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363|1=Zenizuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985|1=Ōtorizuka Kofun}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900|1=Tentative List of World Heritage Sites}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|1=World Heritage Site}}}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|en|Nakatsuhime|WD=Q11378496|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|1=Nakatsuhime}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}

{{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375|1=Yashimazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367|1=Zen'emon'yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363|1=Zenizuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985|1=Ōtorizuka Kofun}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900|1=Tentative List of World Heritage Sites}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|1=World Heritage Site}}}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|en|Nakatsuhime|WD=Q11378496|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|1=Nakatsuhime}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}
== References ==

These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|en|Nakatsuhime|WD=Q11378496|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|1=Nakatsuhime}}.
