{{ill|Ojin-tenno-ryo Kofun, Konda-maruyama Kofun and Futatsuzuka Kofun|WD=Q66124378|1=Ojin-tenno-ryo Kofun, Konda-maruyama Kofun and Futatsuzuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|WD=Q11471921|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|WD=Q11410610|1=Osamezuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|WD=Q66124366|1=Shichikannon Kofun}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|WD=Q66124373|1=Suketayama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|WD=Q11576219|1=Tadeiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|WD=Q66124356|1=Ryusa-yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|WD=Q66124365|1=Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|WD=Q11555978|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|WD=Q11428445|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|WD=Q66124375|1=Yashimazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|WD=Q66124367|1=Zen'emon'yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|WD=Q66124363|1=Zenizuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11441985|1=Ōtorizuka Kofun}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|WD=Q1459900|1=Tentative List of World Heritage Sites}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|1=World Heritage Site}}}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|en|Nakatsuhime|WD=Q11378496|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|1=Nakatsuhime}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}
== References ==

These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|qid=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|en|Nakatsuhime|qid=Q11378496|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|1=Nakatsuhime}}.

{{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|qid=Q11378467|1=Nakatsuyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Nakayamazuka Kofun|qid=Q66124374|1=Nakayamazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Nishiumazuka Kofun|qid=Q66124383|1=Nishiumazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|qid=Q11645001|1=Nonaka Kofun}}; {{ill|Ojin-tenno-ryo Kofun, Konda-maruyama Kofun and Futatsuzuka Kofun|qid=Q66124378|1=Ojin-tenno-ryo Kofun, Konda-maruyama Kofun and Futatsuzuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Oka Misanzai Kofun|qid=Q11471921|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}}; {{ill|Osamezuka Kofun|qid=Q11410610|1=Osamezuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|qid=Q66124366|1=Shichikannon Kofun}}; {{ill|Suketayama Kofun|qid=Q66124373|1=Suketayama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tadeiyama Kofun|qid=Q11576219|1=Tadeiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tatsusayama Kofun|qid=Q66124356|1=Ryusa-yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Terayama-minamiyama Kofun|qid=Q66124365|1=Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun|qid=Q11555978|1=Tsudō-Shiroyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|qid=Q11428445|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}; {{ill|Yashimazuka Kofun|qid=Q66124375|1=Yashimazuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Zen'emon'yama Kofun|qid=Q66124367|1=Zen'emon'yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Zenizuka Kofun|qid=Q66124363|1=Zenizuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Ōtorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11441985|1=Ōtorizuka Kofun}}|Historic Site={{ill|Tentative World Heritage Site|qid=Q1459900|1=Tentative List of World Heritage Sites}}; {{ill|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259|1=World Heritage Site}}}} '''Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan''' ({{lang|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}) is a collective name for 45 kofun located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|qid=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, and {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. These tumuli are categorized into the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. The site was officially designated as a World Cultural Heritage Site during the {{ill|43rd World Heritage Committee|ja|第43回世界遺産委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/43rd World Heritage Committee|1=43rd Session of the World Heritage Committee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q59384203}} meeting on [[July 6]], [[2019]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190706/k00/00m/040/180000c|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group designated as World Heritage Site|publisher=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-07-06}}</ref>
== Background and Preservation == === Nomination for World Heritage Listing === Efforts to nominate the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group as a World Heritage Site began in 2007 when it was proposed to {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|qid=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. After being included in Japan's tentative list in 2008, it was officially submitted to UNESCO in 2010.<ref>{{Cite book|title=World Heritage Yearbook 2009|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|date=2009}}</ref>
=== Preservation Measures === As many kofun are classified as {{ill|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Mausoleum Reference Land|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, protection under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} is limited. Instead, preservation measures include restrictions on construction and landscape design under urban planning laws.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/conference/headquarters_nine_dl/gidai01.pdf|title=Preservation Measures for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|accessdate=2021-05-09}}</ref>
== Composition == The site comprises 45 kofun in three cities, dating from the late 4th to 5th centuries.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Mozu Area === * {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}: Japan's largest kofun, believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. * {{ill|Nagayama Kofun|ja|永山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayama Kofun|qq=}}: Accompanying tumulus associated with the Daisen Kofun.
=== Notable Tumuli in the Furuichi Area === * {{ill|Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyo-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. * {{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲津山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲ツ山古墳}}: Attributed to {{ill|Nakatsuhime|en|Nakatsuhime|qid=Q11378496|lt=Empress Nakatsuhime|1=Nakatsuhime}}.
== Related Information == * {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|qid=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} official page for Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: [http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/] * {{Cite book|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Heritage and Preservation|author=Sakai City Cultural Property Division|year=2019}}
